label: Wide-shell 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED with HALF-SPINE hall, living-kitchen public band (lk style) — the COMPACT CHAMPION of the wide family: spine runs only over the private east half, public west half is one open lk band. Designed to solve the 11.0 x 7.5 m BP 220 shell and down to ~10.5 x 7.2 m (76 m²).

adjacencies.note: lk style — living (front-west, hosts entry) and kitchen (rear-west) are one open band; dining folds into the living side
ensuite is an alcove on master's rear edge — interior door from master
master (front-east) doors north into the half-spine
br2 doors south into the half-spine
br3 doors south into the half-spine at its east end
standard pair side-by-side in the rear band — wall only, no door
common T&B doors south into the half-spine at its west end
common backs onto the kitchen — stack 1, one shared plumbing wall
the half-spine's west mouth opens into the living room — 0.9 m minimum single-person mouth (compact envelope cannot afford wider)

soft_proximities.note: ensuite alcove sits across the spine from common — keep them aligned so both branches ride one under-slab run
keep the shared bath beside the standard pair

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_wd_half_spine_baths_ds_hall_lk
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: This topology IS the compact fallback of the wide 3BR family, so its own profile bites late (90 m²) and caps hard: master 13 m², standards 10 m², ensuite capped at 3.5 m² so the living band keeps >= 13 m². Hall floors at 0.95 m. At the 76 m² floor (10.5 x 7.2) everything sits on hard minimums with a ~14 m² living-kitchen — tight but code-legal under BP 220.

notes: 3BR wide catalog topology #3 — HALF-SPINE, the COMPACT CHAMPION and the
declared fallback of #1 (center_spine) and #2 (side_split). Natural floor
band ~76-92 m². Designed against the BP 220 test case: 14.0 x 11.0 m lot
(1.5 m sides/front, 2.0 m rear) → 11.0 x 7.5 m shell (82.5 m²). Solves down
to ~10.5 x 7.2 m (76 m²). No fallback — this is the floor of the family.
Layout intent (canonical, west = left):
Rear:  [kitchen | common T&B | br2  | br3          ]
Spine: [ (open) | -- HALL (0.95 m, east half) ---- ]
Front: [living (entry)      | master     : ensuite ]
Program: same two-band section as center_spine but the corridor only spans
the private east half — the west half is a full-depth open lk band (living
front, kitchen rear, dining folded into living). Cutting the spine's west
run saves ~3.5 m² of circulation, which is exactly what lets this parti
clear 76-82 m² shells where center_spine fails (~88 m² floor).
Circulation: spine mouths west into the living room and serves common, br2,
br3 (north side) and master (south side). Every bedroom still doors onto
circulation, not the LDK — no privacy concession despite the compact size.
Ventilation: all bedrooms + living on front/rear walls; common vents high
window over the service area; NBC Rule 7/8 clean.
Plumbing: kitchen+common wet wall is stack 1; master's ensuite alcove sits
directly across the spine from common (soft proximity keeps them aligned) so
both baths ride one under-slab branch — effectively 1.5 stacks, cheapest
after front_back_split's single stack in the squarish family.
Fit rule: frontage >= 10.5 m (living 4.3 + master 3.3 + br widths behind),
depth >= 7.2 m (3.1 + 0.95 + 3.0 + walls). Carport: NCP base form; on the
14 x 11 test lot use a front-parallel carport in the 1.5 m + street verge or
widen one side setback to 3.0 m and drop shell width to 10.5 m. Mirror when
brief.carport_side='left'.
